question: Install Ansible on the Rocky Linux system using dnf.
hint: Run the command with sudo privileges if needed.

question: Create a playbook named site.yml in the current directory that installs the nginx package using the yum module.
hint: Use the ansible.builtin.yum module with name: nginx and state: present.
validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
if [ -f site.yml ]; then
  if grep -q "name.*nginx" site.yml && grep -q "state.*present" site.yml; then
    exit 0
  fi
fi
exit 1

question: Run the site.yml playbook against localhost to install nginx.
Ensure the nginx service is started and enabled after the playbook run. Run the appropriate command to verify both statuses.
hint: You can run two commands: systemctl is-active nginx and systemctl is-enabled nginx.
validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
out=$(systemctl is-active nginx)
out2=$(systemctl is-enabled nginx)
if [ "$out" = "active" ] && [ "$out2" = "enabled" ]; then exit 0; else exit 1; fi

question: Update the site.yml playbook to also start and enable the nginx service using the service module after installing the package.
hint: Add a task with ansible.builtin.service, name: nginx, state: started, enabled: true.
validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
if [ -f site.yml ]; then
  if grep -q "name.*nginx" site.yml && grep -q "state.*present" site.yml && grep -q "ansible.builtin.service" site.yml && grep -q "state.*started" site.yml && grep -q "enabled.*true" site.yml; then
    exit 0
  fi
fi
exit 1
